rulings.Case Title: JOSE LIZARRAGA VS. SANMO TRAVEL AGENCY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Docs Responsive To Pltf'S Req For Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 13, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF JOSE LIZARRAGA'S Motion To Compel Documents Responsive To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JASON WONG VS. BURLINGAME PROFESSIONAL BUILDING, INC., A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Judgmnt On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT BURLINGAME PROFESSIONAL BUILDING, INC.'s motion For Judgmnt On The Pleadings. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. When considered as a whole and read in the light most favorable to Plaintiff, the first, third and fourth causes of action in the Third Amended Complaint are sufficiently pled. The motion to strike the reference to "Defendant" Barbara Jiang in paragraph 16 of the Third Amended Complaint is granted as unopposed and for good cause shown. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE RALLO-ECKHOFF et al VS. ARCON CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANTs YEVGENIY LIBOV & MARIYA LIBOV's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied in part and granted in part. The motion to strike paragraph five of the Second Amended Complaint is denied. It sufficiently pleads alter ego liability and is not irrelevant. The motion to strike punitive and exemplary damages is denied. They are justified with reference to the fraud causes of action. The motion to strike the fifth and sixth causes of action is denied. While technically new, they are not so far outside the scope of the Court's order as to justify a motion to strike. The motion to strike the eleventh and twelfth causes of action is granted. They are entirely new causes of action, and Plaintiff has not opposed the motion to strike as to these causes of action. Plaintiff must seek leave of Court to amend the complaint to add new causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE RALLO-ECKHOFF et al VS. ARCON CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANTs YEVGENIY LIBOV & MARIYA LIBOV's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled alter ego liability in the first and second causes of action. Leek v Cooper (2011) 194 Cal. App. 4th 399, does not provide otherwise. Defendant's factual objections to the fifth and sixth causes of action are inappropriate on demurrer. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled that Defendant knew of the defects in the home and did not disclose them. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to support the seventh, eighth, and ninth causes of action based on fraud. Plaintiff points to specific misrepresentations made by Defendant to induce Plaintiff to purchase the property. Defendant's demurrers to the eleventh and twelfth causes of action for emotional distress are moot based on the Court's ruling on the motion to strike. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND FUNDING LLC VS. CHRISTOPH DESOTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Midland Funding Llc'S Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Req. For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 13, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 7, Plaintiff Midland Funding LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, for reasons set forth in plaintiff moving party's papers; defendant Christoph Soto has not filed any opposition to the motion, nor provided any response to the discovery. Defendant is ordered to provide serve plaintiff with verified responses to plaintiff's Request For Production of Documents, Set One, without objections, within 15 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: JUVENTINO ESTRADA VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013, Line 20, PLAINTIFF JUVENTINO ESTRADA's Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents. Granted in part and denied in part. Request Nos. 13-15: Granted to the extent that National Union shall provide a written verification that it has provided all responsive documents within its possession, custody or control that it has been able to locate following a diligent search. As Request No. 5 and request nos. 19-25, 27-28: Denied. The Master Policy has been produced. The low relevance of the requested documents is outweighed by the burden to National Union of providing these additional documents, without any real basis to do so. The remainder of the motion is denied. No sanctions are awarded to either party. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: REGENCY CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC VS. ELAJOU GROUP, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANTs ELAJOU GROUP, LLC & JUMA ELAJOU's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. As to the first, fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action, the demurrer is overruled because it is not apparent from the face of the complaint that Plaintiff is required to be licensed as a real estate broker. Plaintiff has pled that it acted only as a finder. As to the second cause of action, the demurrer is overruled because the guarantee is supported by a writing in the form of an email. As to the third cause of action, the demurrer is overruled because the complaint contains sufficient facts to meet the heightened pleading requirements for fraud. rulings.Case Title: GCA SAVVIAN ADVISORS, LLC VS. VIOLIN MEMORY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT GCA SAVVIAN ADVISORS, LLC's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. The Court takes judicial notice of the contract under Evid. 452(h). The contract does not conclusively show that Violin fails to state a cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. Whether the parties shared a fiduciary relationship is a factual issue based on the parties' conduct. GCA also reads paragraph 6 of the contract as a clause that immunizes it from liability. That reading violates CC 1668. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CONNELLY VS. COLLEEN HOLLAND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Issuance Of A Preliminary Injunction And Appointment Of Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT COLLEEN HOLLAND's Motion For Issuance Of A Preliminary Injunction And Appointment Of Receiver. Hearing required. The Court's tentative view is to grant the motion to appoint receiver, and deny the motion for preliminary injunction as unnecessary. Ms. Holland demonstrates that she is a co-owner of the business and Mr. Connelly has been misusing his position to her detriment. A receiver is necessary to protect her property rights. See CCP 564(b)(9); Neider v. Dardi (1955) 130 Cal.App.2d 646, 649. The Court is inclined to appoint a receiver with broad powers to manage the business and restrain Mr. Connelly from taking advantage of his position as a co-owner. The parties are to meet and confer regarding the identity of and appointment of a receiver. See Cal. Rules of Court 3.1177; 3.1178. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK N.A. VS. IRINA ZINCHIK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Or In The Alternative, To Compel Further Responses To The Same And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 13, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 6, DEFENDANT IRINA ZINCHIK'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Or In The Alternative, To Compel Further Responses To The Same And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT VS. 1015 PIERCE STREET INVESTORS LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013, Line 14, PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Continued to March 20, 2013 to give the moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must immediately deliver courtesy copies of its moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROGER PORTER VS. SANMO INTERNATIONAL COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANTs LARISA DRAGUNSKAYA & SAMMO INTERNATIONAL COMPANY, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the first cause of action for negligence per se and overruled as to the remaining causes of action. Plaintiff must allege the statutory basis for asserting his negligence per se claims. The remaining causes of action are adequately pled. The allegations are not uncertain. The Court does not have a courtesy copy of the Reply brief. This must be brought to Court tomorrow at 9:15 a.m. If possible, it should be sent electronically this afternoon to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROGER PORTER VS. SANMO INTERNATIONAL COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANTs LARISA DRAGUNSKAYA & SAMMO INTERNATIONAL COMPANY, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted. Defendants have met their burden to show that the defamation claims are at least partially based on protected activity, that is, statements made to the EDD. (Dible v. Haight Ashbury Free Clinics (2009) 170 Cal.App.4th 843, 850.) The Complaint at 5 is incorporated by reference into the defamation cause of action. The burden shifted to Plaintiff to show his probability of prevailing on the merits on either protected or non-protected activity as the defamation cause of action. Plaintiff has not provided sufficient evidence to show that he will prevail on his defamation claims. Plaintiff submits what is alleged to be the declaration Defendant Dragunskaya. (Panto Decl. 3, Ex. A.) There is no evidence as to the context of this declaration: whether it was in connection with the EDD proceeding or whether it was ever actually published to anyone. Plaintiff has presented no other evidence to establish a probability of prevailing on the merits. Reasonable attorneys fees and costs will be awarded to Defendant. The Court does not have a courtesy copy of the Reply brief. This must be brought to Court tomorrow at 9:15 a.m. If possible, it should be sent electronically this afternoon to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CACH, LLC VS. RONALD P FARNSWORTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT RONALD FARNSWORTH's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with 15 days leave to amend. The Court did not receive Plaintiff's courtesy copy of its opposition (as required by San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B and the Court's order of February 8, 2013) until March 12, 2013. . (The envelope containing the courtesy copy is postmarked March 7, 2013.) On February 8, 2013, the Court ordered that courtesy copies must be provided by February 15, 2013. Plaintiff failed to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
